message:
test: add ~48 edge-case tests across fuse, ondisk-ext4, and types crates

Hardens three crates with targeted edge-case coverage:
- ffs-fuse: 19 tests (MountOptions, MetricsSnapshot, AccessPredictor, ReadaheadManager, FuseError, CacheLinePadded)
- ffs-ondisk ext4: 20 tests (checksums, extents, timestamps, permissions, dir entries, feature flags, rec_len, inode flags)
- ffs-types: 9 tests (DeviceId, SimdCapabilities, Display, Ordering, BlockSize shifts, overflow)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

DegradationLevel::from_raw, label, should_pause_background, should_reduce_cache, should_throttle_writes, should_read_only have zero direct tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:31:33.654084079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:32:34.394358785Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T11:32:34.394256543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2lpe","title":"Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible","description":"# Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible

## GOAL
Minimize data copies on I/O paths, applying mechanical sympathy principles.

## BACKGROUND
FUSE fundamentally requires kernel/userspace copies. But we can minimize userspace copies:
1. Direct buffer handoff between cache and FUSE
2. Vectored I/O for multi-block operations
3. Page-aligned allocations for OS optimization

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. BlockBuf Refcounting
```rust
pub struct BlockBuf {
    data: Arc<AlignedVec>,  // Shared, aligned buffer
}

impl BlockBuf {
    pub fn as_slice(&self) -> &[u8];
    pub fn clone_ref(&self) -> BlockBuf;  // Arc::clone
    pub fn make_mut(&mut self) -> &mut [u8]; // COW if shared
}
```

### 2. Aligned Allocation
```rust
pub struct AlignedVec {
    ptr: NonNull<u8>,
    len: usize,
    cap: usize,
}

impl AlignedVec {
    pub fn new(size: usize, align: usize) -> Self;
    // Align to page size (4096) for direct I/O compatibility
}
```

### 3. Vectored I/O
```rust
pub trait VectoredBlockDevice: BlockDevice {
    fn read_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &mut [BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
    
    fn write_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &[BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
}
```

### 4. FUSE Buffer Handoff
- Use fuser reply buffer directly where possible
- Avoid intermediate Vec allocations
- Consider mmap for large reads (if FUSE supports)

## TESTS
1. Unit: BlockBuf refcounting semantics
2. Unit: Aligned allocation alignment
3. Integration: Vectored I/O correctness
4. Benchmark: Copy count measurement

## LOGGING
- Buffer allocation (trace): size, alignment
- Copy detected (trace): source, dest, size
- Vectored I/O (trace): block count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. No unnecessary copies in hot paths
2. Memory usage not increased
3. Performance improvement measurable
4. Correctness preserved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:26.339499830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291811560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291782436Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","optimization","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.318748579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.832494790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2oa","title":"ffs-error: Decide mount-validation error variants (UnsupportedFeature/UnsupportedBlockSize/etc)","description":"Context: mount validation can fail because of unsupported feature bits, block sizes, or incompatible on-disk geometry.

Question: do we represent these as:
- ParseError::InvalidField (and map to FfsError::Format), OR
- dedicated FfsError variants (UnsupportedFeature, UnsupportedBlockSize, InvalidGeometry), OR
- an intermediate MountValidationError used by ffs-core.

Deliverables:
- Chosen approach documented.
- Error mapping yields correct errno (EINVAL vs EOPNOTSUPP) for FUSE and CLI.

Acceptance:
- ext4 validate_v1() failures are surfaced with stable errors and good UX.
- Docs/spec do not list non-existent FfsError variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:59.187355332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423539784Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423519876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-2oah","title":"EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)","description":"# EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)

## PURPOSE
Scale WAL (Write-Ahead Log) throughput for concurrent MVCC writers by using per-core WAL buffers with epoch-based ordering. Current single WAL is a serialization bottleneck for concurrent transactions.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 15.4:
- Silo (Tu et al., SOSP 2013): per-core log buffers, epoch-based commit ordering
- Aether (Johnson et al., VLDB 2010): decoupled log buffer management, early lock release
- Key insight: WAL serialization is unnecessary if epoch ordering provides equivalent guarantees

## DESIGN

### Per-Core WAL Buffers
- Each CPU core has a dedicated WAL buffer (thread-local or core-affinity)
- Transactions write to their core's buffer without contention
- No central lock on WAL append

### Epoch-Based Ordering
- Global epoch counter advanced periodically (e.g., every 10ms or every N commits)
- Each WAL entry stamped with current epoch
- Durability guarantee: all entries in epoch E are durable before epoch E+1 begins
- Recovery: replay all entries in epoch order (entries within same epoch are commutative)

### Group Commit
- All WAL buffers for epoch E flushed together (group commit)
- Single fsync for all cores' buffers at epoch boundary
- Reduces fsync overhead from O(transactions) to O(epochs)

### Recovery
- On crash: find last complete epoch across all per-core logs
- Replay all entries up to last complete epoch
- Discard entries from incomplete epoch (they never committed)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Per-core WAL buffers eliminate contention
- [ ] Epoch-based ordering provides equivalent durability guarantees
- [ ] Group commit reduces fsync frequency
- [ ] Recovery correctly handles incomplete epochs
- [ ] Benchmark: WAL throughput scales with core count

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E WAL test (bd-2oah.4) passes
3. Parallel WAL scales linearly to 4+ cores
4. Group commit reduces fsync count by >= 50pct
5. Crash recovery replays all committed transactions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:54.753437422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641784818Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641711741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","durability","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:40.817042970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-2oah","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-2s4.5","title":"E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency","description":"# E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency

## GOAL
Verify that FrankenFS maintains consistency guarantees after an unclean shutdown (SIGKILL during active writes). The filesystem must be mountable, crash recovery must work, and all committed data must be intact.

## TEST SCENARIO

### Phase 1: Setup
1. Create fresh 256MB ext4 image
2. Mount via FUSE, write baseline dataset (500 files, checksums recorded)
3. fsync all files, verify baseline is durable
4. Begin continuous write workload (background thread writing new files)

### Phase 2: Crash
5. After 2 seconds of active writing, send SIGKILL to FUSE process
6. Record which files had fsync called vs which were in-flight

### Phase 3: Recovery
7. Remount the filesystem image
8. Crash recovery should trigger automatically (journal replay, MVCC rollback)
9. Record evidence ledger output

### Phase 4: Verification
10. All baseline files (pre-crash) must be intact with correct checksums
11. Files that had fsync completed must be intact
12. Files that were in-flight at SIGKILL time: acceptable to be missing or truncated, but:
    - Must NOT contain garbage data
    - Must NOT corrupt other files
    - Directory must be consistent (no dangling entries)
13. No filesystem-level corruption (fsck-equivalent check passes)

### Invariants
- Atomicity: a file write either fully committed or fully absent
- No cross-file corruption: crash during file A write must never affect file B
- Metadata consistency: directory entries match existing inodes
- Journal consistency: no partial journal transactions visible

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Filesystem mounts after SIGKILL without errors
- [ ] All pre-crash baseline files intact with correct checksums
- [ ] All fsync'd files intact
- [ ] In-flight files are either complete or cleanly absent (no partial garbage)
- [ ] No cross-file corruption
- [ ] Evidence ledger records crash recovery actions
- [ ] Test is reproducible (deterministic write pattern)
- [ ] Test completes in < 60s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682290287Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682269679Z","close_reason":"Completed: SIGKILL E2E now passes with deterministic crash invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","e2e","fuse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:10.730235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:13.504998589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:08:10.911533057Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

New crash phase now: (1) writes+fsyncs configurable baseline dataset (default 500 files), (2) runs continuous in-flight writer with event log, (3) SIGKILLs mount daemon after configurable runtime, (4) remounts RO, (5) verifies baseline checksums + fsync durability invariants + non-fsync clean-prefix/no-garbage behavior, and (6) checks remount log for crash-recovery evidence. Validation run: bash -n passes. rch offloaded gates attempted; cargo check/clippy/test all fail remotely with pre-existing libc resolution conflict from /dp/asupersync (libc=0.2.180) vs ffs-core (libc ^0.2.181).","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:33:04Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"OliveSummit","text":"Updated scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh to run mount/inspect through local FFS_CLI_BIN (default target/release/ffs-cli) while still building via cargo (offloadable through rch). Verified syntax with bash -n and ran full script using rch-offloaded build path. Run skipped at mount phase due fuse3 allow_other policy (user_allow_other not enabled in /etc/fuse.conf), with explicit diagnostic captured in artifacts/e2e/20260216_104540_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log and mount_rw_mnt_rw.log.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:46:59Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"OliveSummit","text":"Added mount-permission phase gate to scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh: explicit SKIP when fusermount reports Permission denied / Operation not permitted. Also added FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT env support in crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (default true, accepts 1/0/true/false/yes/no/on/off) and set script default FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 to avoid implicit allow_other injection. Validation: rch exec cargo fmt --all --check, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings all passed. Full script run now exits 0 with clear skip diagnostic on this host-level FUSE denial: artifacts/e2e/20260216_104953_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/{run.log,mount_rw_mnt_rw.log}.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:50:13Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"OliveSummit","text":"Privileged validation attempt using sudo reached RW mount path. So this bead is unblocked on clean-remount durability and now focused on SIGKILL/fsync crash-consistency invariant gaps.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:36Z"},{"id":240,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completion update:

Resolved two blockers and validated end-to-end:

1) Clean unmount/remount durability blocker (from `bd-2s4.7`):
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`: RW mount now opens ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::Apply` (RO stays `SimulateOverlay`).
- Added unit test `ext4_mount_replay_mode_is_persistent_for_rw`.

2) SIGKILL verifier false-positive in this bead:
- `scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`: crash verifier now keys strict durability on actual `fsync_done` markers, not planned filename mode. For planned-but-unfsynced files, clean prefix truncation is allowed.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ffs-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- `bash -n scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh` ✅
- Privileged full E2E pass:
  `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh` ✅

Evidence artifacts:

 {"id":"bd-2s4.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Production FUSE Runtime epic (bd-2s4). Currently the epic has E2E tests (bd-2s4.4, bd-2s4.5, bd-3en1) but NO dedicated unit tests for the dispatch layer, lifecycle management, or errno mapping.

## TEST PLAN

### 1. Dispatch Tests
- Thread pool spawns correct number of threads
- Concurrent requests are dispatched without deadlock
- Request timeout fires and returns EIO
- Queue depth metrics are updated correctly

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Mount initializes all subsystems in correct order
- Unmount flushes dirty data before teardown
- SIGTERM triggers clean unmount sequence
- Double-unmount is idempotent (no panic)
- Mount with invalid image returns descriptive error

### 3. Errno Mapping Tests
- Every FfsError variant maps to correct errno
- Unknown errors map to EIO
- Cancelled maps to EINTR
- CorruptData maps to EIO
- NoSpace maps to ENOSPC
- PermissionDenied maps to EACCES

### 4. State Tests
- FsOps state is Send + Sync (compile-time test)
- Concurrent getattr calls do not race
- Cache access under lock is bounded (no starvation)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. All tests listed above
2. Total: ~20 unit tests
3. All deterministic, no I/O (mock BlockDevice)

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full context (operation, expected, actual)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All dispatch, lifecycle, errno, and state tests pass
2. No flaky tests (deterministic)
3.  {"id":"bd-2s4.7","title":"Fix ext4 RW write persistence across clean unmount/remount","description":"## Summary
In privileged E2E runs, ext4 RW operations succeed, but data written in phase 3 is missing after clean unmount + RO remount.

## Repro
1. Build `ffs-cli` (or use prebuilt binary).
2. Run:
   `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
3. Observe failure in phase 4: `persist.txt` is missing on RO remount.

## Evidence
- Run artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`
- RO mount log: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/mount_ro_mnt_ro.log`

## Expected
Writes acknowledged in RW mount (and fsync-backed sentinel writes) remain visible after clean unmount/remount.

## Actual
`persist.txt` is readable in RW mount, but absent after remount.

## Notes
- Prior RW EIO mount failure appears resolved in this environment.
- Unmount robustness was improved in `scripts/e2e/lib.sh`, so this now consistently surfaces a persistence/durability issue rather than mount teardown noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625109148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625071358Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed RW persistence loss by using Apply replay mode for RW mount","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","ext4","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":238,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolved root cause in CLI mount path: `mount_cmd` was always opening ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::SimulateOverlay`, which made RW writes session-local and non-persistent.

Change:
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`: added `ext4_mount_replay_mode(read_write)` and switched RW mount to `Apply` while keeping RO mount on `SimulateOverlay`.
- Added unit test `ext4_mount_replay_mode_is_persistent_for_rw`.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ffs-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- Privileged E2E run: `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
  - Phase 4 now passes: `persist.txt` and appended `mtime-check` survive clean unmount + RO remount.
  - Artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_041747_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2t1","title":"MVCC: Specify API + invariants (TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot) and phase plan (FCW -> SSI)","description":"Goal: make MVCC semantics unambiguous before integrating with filesystem operations.

Deliverables:
- Document: what a Snapshot means (visibility window), when it is taken, and how it is used for reads.
- Document: transaction lifecycle (begin -> stage writes -> commit/abort) and what is allowed concurrently.
- Phase plan:
  - Phase A: First-committer-wins (FCW) conflict check at commit.
  - Phase B: Add optional SSI tracking (read sets + rw-antidependencies) where needed.

Acceptance:
- ffs-mvcc public API is stable enough that ffs-fuse can build on it.
- There is a simple correctness proof sketch for FCW and a roadmap for SSI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:27.695269767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:36.304894733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:36.304874716Z","close_reason":"Spec: clarify MVCC Snapshot/Transaction lifecycle; Phase A (FCW) vs Phase B (SSI) plan; document Phase A API","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc"]}

 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
